commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=bb4d01efab32fba36765e7bf007e921152fd9dfa)
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes update dry-run exact semver tags probe the npm registry before reporting `targetVersion`, adds an unpublished-target note, and covers the helper with focused Vitest tests.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +47. Total +59 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection and the linked production observation: current main returns exact semver tags directly in dry-run while apply installs `openclaw@<tag>` through npm. I did not live-reproduce the original propagation window because `2026.5.26` now returns 200 from the npm registry.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and one small review cleanup; this is not a ClawSweeper repair lane while proof is mock-only.

target.title: fix(update): warn when exact package tag is unpublished
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes `openclaw update --dry-run --tag <exact-version>` so exact npm version targets are verified against the npm registry before dry-run reports a resolved `targetVersion`.
- If the exact version is not published yet, dry-run now leaves `targetVersion` unresolved and adds an explicit note that apply will fail until npm propagation completes.
- Keeps non-registry specs and transient registry failures usable, so GitHub/package-spec previews do not become brittle.

## Linked context
Closes #87320

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: dry-run could report an exact version target as resolved even when npm would reject apply with `ETARGET`/notarget.
- Real environment tested: source-level OpenClaw CLI update helper in a fresh worktree from current `origin/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `corepack pnpm test src/cli/update-cli/shared.command-runner.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/cli/update-cli/shared.ts src/cli/update-cli/shared.command-runner.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix: the focused Vitest file passed with 5 tests, including a regression where `fetchNpmPackageTargetStatus()` returns `HTTP 404` for `2026.5.26` and `resolveTargetVersion("2026.5.26")` returns `null` instead of treating the semver string as resolved.
- Observed result after fix: exact semver dry-run resolution now depends on npm registry availability; inconclusive registry probes still fall back to the parsed version to avoid blocking dry-run on transient network errors.
- What was not tested: full update apply against a live unpublished npm version, because the historical propagation window has passed and applying updater mutations would be inappropriate in this contributor environment.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is covered with focused unit behavior rather than a live updater mutation.

## Tests and validation
- `corepack pnpm test src/cli/update-cli/shared.command-runner.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` passed.
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/cli/update-cli/shared.ts src/cli/update-cli/shared.command-runner.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts` completed.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`
Highest-risk area: dry-run now performs a registry probe for exact semver tags. The fallback keeps dry-run usable if the probe is inconclusive, while the known `HTTP 404` case no longer gives a false green light.

## Current review state
Ready for maintainer review and CI.
target.author: charles-openclaw
